sales.description:
Ancients
EGYPT. Alexandria. Tiberius (AD 14-37). BI tetradrachm (24mm, 13.92 gm, 12h). NGC MS 5/5 - 3/5. Dated Regnal Year 7 (AD 20/21). ΤΙΒΕΡΙΟΣ ΚΑΙΣΑΡ ΣΕΒΑΣΤΟΣ, laureate head of Tiberius right; L Ζ (date) below chin / ΘΕΟΣ-ΣΕΒΑΣΤΟΣ, radiate head of Divus Augustus right. RPC I 5089. Emmett 60. Dattari 78.

Ex Heritage Auctions, Auction 3098 (18 January 2022), lot 33245; Heritage Auctions, Auction 3051 (8 January 2017), lot 30151.

sales.title: Ira & Larry Goldberg Coins & Collectibles - Auction 150, Lot 585 — Augustus, 27 BC-14 AD. Silver Tetradrachm (15.06g), 25 mm. Struck in Syria, Antioch, Yr. 28 & Cos 12 (4/3 BC)  Laur. bare bust of Augustus facing r. Reverse:  Tyche seated l. holding palm with Orontes
sales.description:
Augustus, 27 BC-14 AD. Silver Tetradrachm (15.06g), 25 mm. Struck in Syria, Antioch, Yr. 28 & Cos 12 (4/3 BC)  Laur. bare bust of Augustus facing r. Reverse:  Tyche seated l. holding palm with Orontes swimming r. NGC graded Choice XF; Strike: 5/5, Surface: 4/5.  Boldly struck and nicely toned. Rare in such choice condition.  Estimated Value $750 - UP
Ex The Peter Preovolos Collection....
